title: Accelerating the Process At Every Step — EMC-Aberdeen Workshop
author: Aberdeen Group (Peter S. Kastner)
abstract: Aberdeen sales-acceleration deck prepared for EMC outlining a research-driven program to pull through storage solutions. Positions Pools of Storage and Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) as the CIO strategic agenda, argues the 'opening gun just went off' on ILM and predicts ILM will be the big storage-related market battle of the decade. Maps Q1 Storage Research findings on cost, consolidation, retention policies, regulation, and ILM.
importance_rationale: Early, widely-circulated industry articulation of the ILM thesis and Pools of Storage concept; shaped EMC's 2004-2010 go-to-market and the broader storage industry's data-lifecycle narrative.
relevance_rationale: ILM/information-governance remains central to storage, backup, compliance, and data-platform decisions; lessons on 'great new ideas short on solutions' apply to current AI-infrastructure narratives.
prescience_rationale: ILM did become the big storage-related market battle of the decade (EMC, IBM Tivoli, HDS, Symantec/NetBackup, CommVault all framed around ILM through 2010s); vagueness-of-definition prediction also verified.
